title: Java Stream Group By
description: Practice grouping stream results into a Map.
learningGoals: Collect stream values into a Map.

completionChecks.prompt: What are the keys in ordersByCustomer after this collector runs?
choices: A running count of all orders
The customer ids returned by Order::customerId
completionChecks.explanation: Collectors.groupingBy uses the classifier function's result as each map key, so every key is a customer id returned by Order::customerId.

completionChecks.prompt: What does each value in the resulting Map<String, List<Order>> contain?
choices: The list of orders that share that customer id
Only the most recent order for that customer
The number of orders as an Integer
Compilation fails because groupingBy requires a downstream collector
completionChecks.explanation: Single-argument groupingBy collects each group's elements into a List, so every value is the List<Order> for that customer id; a downstream collector is optional.

completionChecks.prompt: What does ordersByCustomer.get("A").size() return if orders has two customerId "A" orders and one customerId "B" order?
completionChecks.explanation: groupingBy puts all elements with the same classifier key into the same list, so customer A has two orders.
